versions.title: Drive Analytics, Map Layers & Unit Support

changes.description: FSD Analytics panel — click the stats panel to see a more detailed view of Full Self-Driving, Autopilot, TACC, and Manual miles, plus total disengagements and accelerator overrides.
Color-coded FSD Score in the stats panel — red for low, green for high, with a smooth gradient between.
Drive Timeline for browsing drives chronologically.
Google Maps and Satellite base layers — switch from the layer control in the top-right of the map.
Imperial / Metric unit toggle in Settings.
Added toggle for showing FSD markers (disengagements and accelerator overrides) on drives.
Added stats for specific drives.
Added drive timeline with scrubbing.
Confirmation dialogs added to Check Drives and Revert Drives to avoid accidental triggers.
Processing runs on the app's bundled Node runtime — no separate Node.js install required.
Process New Drives no longer fails silently on installed builds.
Direction arrow no longer spins full circles when switching between drive and reverse.
Clicking the map while viewing a specific drive no longer returns you to the overview.
Dismissing the Changelog modal no longer closes the Settings modal behind it.
Switching drives during playback no longer leaves the previous replay running, which could cause the new drive to continue playing through a pause.
FSD, Autopilot, and Manual percentages are now rounded to whole numbers.
Selecting a drive from the list no longer scrolls it behind the sticky date header.
Tag edits inside the stats panel no longer collapse the FSD Analytics view.

versions.title: Update Modal Preview and Confirmation Modals

changes.description: Update Available modal now previews the incoming version's changelog entry.
ALPHA status appended to Check Drives confirmation.
Replay play/pause button restyled as a rounded square to match the rest of the UI.
Switching drives during playback no longer leaves the previous replay interval running, which could cause the new drive to continue playing through a pause.
Color-coded FSD Score in the stats panel — red for low, green for high, with a smooth gradient between.
Click the stats panel to expand an FSD Analytics view — donut chart breaking down Full Self-Driving, Autopilot, TACC, and Manual miles, plus total disengagements and accelerator overrides.
Selecting a specific drive swaps the aggregate stats panel for that drive's own stats and breakdown; back to overview restores the aggregate view.
Drive date/time header and tags moved into the stats panel — sidebar is cleaner, editing stays in place as you switch drives.
Timeline is now visually merged with the bottom edge of the stats panel as a single dock at the bottom of the map.
Current-time label floats directly above the timeline thumb and tracks with it as you scrub or play.
Stats panel cleanup — removed the Clips count, renamed “Miles” to “Miles Driven”.
Confirmation dialog icons are now filled rounded squares instead of outlined circles, matching the rest of the UI.
Settings → Map → new toggle for showing FSD markers (disengagements and accelerator overrides) on drives.
Renamed “Accel Overrides” to “Accelerator Overrides” in the stats panel.
FSD, Autopilot, and Manual percentages are rounded to whole numbers.
Selecting a drive from the list no longer scrolls it behind the sticky date header.
Tag edits inside the stats panel no longer toggle the expanded view.
Added Google Maps and Satellite base layers — switch from the new layer control in the top-right of the map.
Imperial / Metric unit toggle in Settings.
Window size and maximized state are remembered across launches.
Relocated drive timeline to above the overall drive stats.
Added confirmation checks to Check Drives and Revert Drives buttons to avoid accidental triggers.
Settings restyled — toggle pills, compact Imperial/Metric switch.
Overview drive lines are now smoothed out.
Fixed bug related to Play/Pause button not working.
Arrow no longer spins full circles at certain points or when switching between drive and reverse.
Clicking on the map while viewing a specific drive no longer kicks you back to the overview.
Dismissing the Change Log modal no longer also closes the Settings modal behind it.
Process New Drives no longer fails silently on installed builds.
Processing runs on the app’s bundled Node runtime — no separate Node.js install required.
Drive Timeline view for browsing drives chronologically.
Beta update channel — enroll in Settings to receive pre-release builds.
Processing pipeline split into separate, reusable functions.
Process Tesla dashcam clips into drive-data.json.
View drives on an interactive map with route overlays.
GPS repair for gaps in dashcam logs using OSRM routing.
Drive tagging for organization and filtering.
